mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.4% as of 2016-09-15 indicates sustained low-rate regime
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144,747 thousand as of 2016-09-15 indicates near-threshold employment level
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained job creation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150,000 persons, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in retail sales and housing starts signals accelerating consumer demand and construction activity that boosts corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 459,821 million and HOUST at 1,142 thousand as of 2016-08-15
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with synchronized demand expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of co-positive RSAFS-HOUST months.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 104.0 index points signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength that supports corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.4016 as of 2016-08-15 indicates above-threshold production level
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with stable supply conditions
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 52% of instances where INDPRO > 104.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as equity investors anticipate earnings pressure.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% as of 2016-09-15 indicates near-threshold unemployment
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and rising federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.2% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 240.305 and FEDFUNDS at 0.4% as of 2016-09-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of co-triggered tightening instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2.0% annualized signals economic deceleration that reduces corporate profit growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reprrice earnings multiples downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 2.0% in latest quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,583.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2016-04-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production decline combined with rising unemployment signals stagflationary pressure that erodes corporate margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when both conditions occur simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.5% AND UNRATE month-over-month change > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.4016 and UNRATE at 5.0% as of 2016-09-15
regime_hint: stagflationary regime with supply contraction and labor weakness
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 52% of co-triggered stagflation instances.
